goal: Land two coordinated pure-deterministic merge/provenance features in `crates/ucil-core/`: (1) F10 conflict-resolution `pub fn resolve_conflict()` extending the existing `fusion.rs` module with the source-authority hierarchy `LSP/AST > SCIP > KG > text` per master-plan §18 line 1811 (Phase 3 Week 10 deliverable #4) — soft guidance with confidence metadata, unresolvable conflicts surfaced as multi-candidate envelopes; AND (2) F12 multi-tier query merging `pub fn merge_across_tiers()` in net-new `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` per master-plan §17 directory-structure line 1636 + §12.3 hot/warm/cold tier semantics + §11.3 pull-based-relevance + §18 line 1813 (Phase 3 Week 10 deliverable #6) — hot_staging + warm + cold tier results merged into a single response with tier-provenance metadata + confidence-band tag (HOT 0.2-0.4 / WARM 0.5-0.7 / COLD 0.8-1.0). Both ship with frozen-test-at-module-root selectors per DEC-0007 and pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract per the now-9-WO-deep mature pattern (WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073/0083). Bundles two coordinated features around the shared §5.4-merge / §11-tiering / §12.3-confidence-band substrate per the QUADRUPLE-validated bundle-coordinated-features template (WO-0067 classifier+reason-parser, WO-0068 cross-group executor+fusion, WO-0070 G3 parallel-merge, WO-0083 G4 architecture MCP tools). Production-wiring of real tier sources (`HotStagingSource`/`WarmTierSource`/`ColdTierSource` impls reading from `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_*` / future warm-processor outputs / `memory.db` cold table) is OUT OF SCOPE per the established `<Group>Source`-trait-with-test-impl-now / production-impl-later split (G1Source WO-0047, G2 sources WO-0050+0051, G3Source WO-0070, G4Source WO-0073/0083); this WO ships the pure-deterministic merger surface fully self-contained via in-test `TestTierSource` impls (analogous to TestG3Source / TestG4Source) so downstream consumers can wire real tier readers in a follow-up WO without touching the merger.
plan_summary: Two-feature bundle around the shared `ucil-core` merge/provenance substrate (mirroring the WO-0067 ceqp.rs+fusion.rs / WO-0068 cross_group.rs / WO-0070 g3_merge.rs / WO-0083 server.rs+executor.rs handler bundle templates). (1) Extend `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (+~150-200 LOC) with a new `pub enum SourceAuthority { LspAst, Scip, Kg, Text }` (Ord-derived so `LspAst < Scip < Kg < Text` for the ranking-by-priority semantics — the most-authoritative variant has the smallest discriminant), a `pub struct ConflictCandidate<T>` holding `{ value: T, source: SourceAuthority, confidence: f64 }`, and `pub fn resolve_conflict<T: Clone + PartialEq>(candidates: &[ConflictCandidate<T>]) -> ConflictResolution<T>` returning `Resolved { value, source, confidence }` when one candidate strictly dominates by source-authority OR `Unresolvable { candidates: Vec<...>, reason: String }` when multiple candidates tie at the highest authority tier with non-equal values (per master-plan §18 line 1811 'agent-based with source authority as soft guidance'). Frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_conflict_resolution()` at module root in `fusion.rs`. (2) Net-new `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` (+~250-300 LOC) with `pub enum Tier { Hot, Warm, Cold }`, `pub struct TieredResult<T> { value: T, tier: Tier, confidence: f64, observed_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc> }` (or `std::time::SystemTime` if chrono is not in workspace deps yet — executor checks Cargo.toml and uses whichever is already canonical), `pub struct MergedResult<T> { value: T, source_tier: Tier, confidence: f64, contributing_tiers: Vec<Tier> }`, and `pub fn merge_across_tiers<T: Clone + PartialEq>(hot: &[TieredResult<T>], warm: &[TieredResult<T>], cold: &[TieredResult<T>]) -> Vec<MergedResult<T>>`. Merge semantics per §12.3 + §11.3: dedup by value, prefer the most-recent observation when tiers carry the same value (per §11.3 pull-based-relevance — newer hot-tier observation supersedes older cold-tier on the SAME value), tag every merged entry with the primary `source_tier` (the tier whose observation won) AND `contributing_tiers` (deduplicated list of all tiers that surfaced this value, sorted by tier ordinal). Confidence band tagged per §12.3: HOT [0.2, 0.4], WARM [0.5, 0.7], COLD [0.8, 1.0]. Unique values from any tier are surfaced exactly once (no accidental triplication when tiers all carry the same observation). Frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_multi_tier_query_merge()` at module root in `tier_merger.rs`. (3) `pub mod tier_merger;` declaration in `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` placed alphabetically between `schema_migration;` and `types;` per the WO-0067/0068 alphabetical-`pub mod` precedent at `lib.rs:10-17`. Public re-export of `tier_merger::{Tier, TieredResult, MergedResult, merge_across_tiers}` AND `fusion::{SourceAuthority, ConflictCandidate, ConflictResolution, resolve_conflict}` is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO (deferred to the consumer WO that wires tier sources + conflict-resolution into the daemon-side response-assembly pipeline — analogous to WO-0067's deferred re-exports per DEC-0005). (4) Pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract — one mutation per feature (see scope_in #15-#16). Single feat commit lands the F10 surface (fusion.rs delta + frozen test) AND the F12 surface (tier_merger.rs + lib.rs mod decl + frozen test) as ONE cohesive unit per DEC-0005 module-coherence (the M1/M2 mutation-restoration contract requires both surfaces to coexist in one diff; splitting per-feature would produce stub-shaped intermediate states — F12 module without lib.rs declaration would not compile, F10 fn without test would not exercise the authority-precedence). Estimated total LOC: ~600-800 (F10 ~150-200 LOC + F12 ~300-400 LOC + tests ~200 LOC including TestTierSource helper).
scope_in: 1. Extend `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` with the F10 conflict-resolution surface. Add a `pub enum SourceAuthority { LspAst, Scip, Kg, Text }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]`. The discriminant order MUST be `LspAst = 0` < `Scip = 1` < `Kg = 2` < `Text = 3` so the natural `Ord` produces `LspAst < Scip < Kg < Text` — the LOWEST discriminant is the MOST authoritative source (mirror the §18 line 1811 hierarchy 'LSP/AST > SCIP > KG > text'). Add `pub struct ConflictCandidate<T> { pub value: T, pub source: SourceAuthority, pub confidence: f64 }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]` and a doc comment citing master-plan §18 line 1811 verbatim. Add `pub enum ConflictResolution<T> { Resolved { value: T, source: SourceAuthority, confidence: f64 }, Unresolvable { candidates: Vec<ConflictCandidate<T>>, reason: String } }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`.

2. Add `pub fn resolve_conflict<T: Clone + PartialEq>(candidates: &[ConflictCandidate<T>]) -> ConflictResolution<T>` in `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs`. Empty input returns `ConflictResolution::Unresolvable { candidates: vec![], reason: "empty input".to_string() }`. Single-candidate input returns `Resolved` with that candidate's value/source/confidence. Multi-candidate input: find the highest-priority `SourceAuthority` (smallest discriminant via `candidates.iter().map(|c| c.source).min().unwrap()`); collect all candidates at that priority; if they all carry the same `value` (PartialEq), return `Resolved` with the highest confidence among the tied candidates; if values differ at the top tier, return `Unresolvable` with ALL candidates (preserving lower-tier candidates for the agent-mediator surface per master-plan §18 line 1811 'agent-based with source authority as soft guidance' — the resolver is deterministic; the agent layer reads the unresolvable surface). Confidence is monotone non-decreasing within a tier (the tied-tier highest-confidence wins). The function is `pub` (NOT `pub(crate)`) so the daemon-side response-assembly consumer can call it. Function MUST be `#[must_use]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` (clippy::pedantic).

3. Add a frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_conflict_resolution()` at MODULE ROOT in `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`). Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_conflict_resolution` MUST resolve uniquely (substring-match without `--exact`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0068 lessons §planner. The test instantiates `ConflictCandidate<&'static str>` candidates and exercises three sub-scenarios with SA-numbered panic messages: (SA1) empty input → `Unresolvable { candidates: [], reason: "empty input" }`; (SA2) single-candidate input `{value="a", source=Kg, confidence=0.5}` → `Resolved { value: "a", source: Kg, confidence: 0.5 }`; (SA3) multi-tier non-conflict where LSP/AST candidate `{value="a", source=LspAst, confidence=0.9}` co-exists with KG candidate `{value="b", source=Kg, confidence=0.6}` → `Resolved { value: "a", source: LspAst, confidence: 0.9 }` (LSP/AST wins by authority); (SA4) tied-top-tier conflict where two candidates both at LspAst carry different values `{value="a", confidence=0.9}` and `{value="b", confidence=0.85}` → `Unresolvable` with BOTH candidates retained AND a non-empty `reason` containing `"tied at tier"` substring; (SA5) tied-top-tier with SAME value `{value="a", source=LspAst, confidence=0.9}` and `{value="a", source=LspAst, confidence=0.92}` → `Resolved { value: "a", source: LspAst, confidence: 0.92 }` (highest confidence within the tier wins); (SA6) cross-tier dominance preservation: candidate `{value="a", source=LspAst, confidence=0.5}` BEATS `{value="b", source=Text, confidence=0.99}` despite the latter's higher confidence — authority-precedence dominates raw confidence per §18 line 1811. Each `assert_eq!` carries a panic body `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` per DEC-0007 + WO-0066/0067/0068/0070/0083 mature pattern.

4. Net-new file `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` for the F12 surface. Module rustdoc cites master-plan §17 line 1636 (`tier_merger.rs` directory-structure entry), §12.3 hot/warm/cold tier semantics + confidence bands, §11.3 pull-based-relevance, §18 line 1813 (Phase 3 Week 10 deliverable #6 'Multi-tier query merging (hot + warm + cold)'). `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. Imports: `std::cmp::Ordering`, `std::time::SystemTime` (or `chrono::DateTime<Utc>` ONLY if `chrono` is already in `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml`'s direct deps — executor MUST check `cargo metadata --format-version 1 -p ucil-core | jq '.packages[0].dependencies[] | select(.name=="chrono")'` BEFORE choosing; if `chrono` is not already a direct dep, use `SystemTime` to avoid pulling a new transitive dep into the workspace-shared `ucil-core` per WO-0067 §executor lessons 'never import a new crate into ucil-core for simple needs'). Use `core::cmp` types only — no async, no IO, no logging, no `regex`.

5. Add `pub enum Tier { Hot, Warm, Cold }` in tier_merger.rs with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]`. Discriminant order: `Hot = 0` < `Warm = 1` < `Cold = 2` so newer/hotter tiers have smaller discriminants. Doc comment cites §12.3 verbatim band ranges. Add `pub struct TieredResult<T> { pub value: T, pub tier: Tier, pub confidence: f64, pub observed_at: SystemTime }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. Add `pub struct MergedResult<T> { pub value: T, pub source_tier: Tier, pub confidence: f64, pub contributing_tiers: Vec<Tier> }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. Doc comment on MergedResult names what each field means: `source_tier` = the tier whose observation won the merge (most recent observation among tiers carrying this value); `confidence` = the confidence carried by the winning observation (NOT recomputed); `contributing_tiers` = deduplicated, ordinal-sorted list of all tiers that surfaced this value (so a value seen in HOT + WARM + COLD ships as `[Hot, Warm, Cold]`).

6. Add `pub fn merge_across_tiers<T: Clone + PartialEq>(hot: &[TieredResult<T>], warm: &[TieredResult<T>], cold: &[TieredResult<T>]) -> Vec<MergedResult<T>>`. Semantics: (a) iterate all three input slices; (b) for each unique value (by `T: PartialEq`), find ALL tiered observations carrying that value across all three input slices; (c) determine the winning observation by `observed_at` recency (most recent SystemTime wins; ties broken by tier-ordinal-ascending — Hot beats Warm beats Cold for SAME `observed_at`); (d) `source_tier` = winning observation's tier; `confidence` = winning observation's confidence (preserve raw confidence per §12.3 — DO NOT collapse to the band midpoint, the tier alone tags the band); `contributing_tiers` = deduplicated tier list across all tiers that surfaced this value, sorted ascending by tier ordinal. Return order: sorted by `source_tier` ascending (Hot first), then by `confidence` descending (highest-confidence first within a tier), then by stable insertion order (preserve input order for ties to maintain deterministic output). Function MUST be `#[must_use]` per clippy::pedantic. NB: clones T on insertion into MergedResult — caller pays the clone cost; `T: PartialEq` is the only bound (NOT `Hash`/`Ord`) so the function works for arbitrary serde-shaped T's. The function MUST be deterministic — no `HashMap` (use `Vec` + linear scan OR `BTreeMap<&T, ...>` only if `T: Ord` is added; with the `T: Clone + PartialEq` bound, linear-scan dedup is canonical).

7. Add a frozen `#[cfg(test)] pub fn test_multi_tier_query_merge()` at MODULE ROOT in `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`). Selector substring-match `cargo test -p ucil-core tier_merger::test_multi_tier_query_merge` MUST resolve uniquely. The test exercises five sub-scenarios with SA-numbered panic messages: (SA1) empty inputs `merge_across_tiers(&[], &[], &[])` → empty `Vec`; (SA2) single-tier input — only HOT carries `{value="x", confidence=0.3}` → result is `[MergedResult { value: "x", source_tier: Hot, confidence: 0.3, contributing_tiers: [Hot] }]`; (SA3) cross-tier same-value: HOT carries `{value="x", confidence=0.3, observed_at=t_recent}`, COLD carries `{value="x", confidence=0.9, observed_at=t_old}` → result is `[MergedResult { value: "x", source_tier: Hot, confidence: 0.3, contributing_tiers: [Hot, Cold] }]` (HOT wins by recency; COLD's higher confidence does NOT override — recency is canonical per §11.3 pull-based-relevance); (SA4) cross-tier different-values: HOT `{value="x", confidence=0.3}` and COLD `{value="y", confidence=0.95}` → result is two entries, ordered by `source_tier` ascending: `[MergedResult { value: "x", source_tier: Hot, confidence: 0.3, contributing_tiers: [Hot] }, MergedResult { value: "y", source_tier: Cold, confidence: 0.95, contributing_tiers: [Cold] }]`; (SA5) all-three-tiers same-value: HOT/WARM/COLD all carry `{value="x"}` with different observed_at — result has ONE entry with `contributing_tiers: [Hot, Warm, Cold]` (deduplicated, ordinal-sorted). Each assertion carries `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic body per the established WO-0066/0067/0068/0070/0083 mature pattern.

8. Add `pub mod tier_merger;` declaration in `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` placed alphabetically between `pub mod schema_migration;` and `pub mod types;` per the existing alphabetical-`pub mod` ordering at `lib.rs:10-17` (verified pattern: ceqp, cross_group, fusion, incremental, knowledge_graph, otel, schema_migration, types — all alphabetical). DO NOT add a `pub use tier_merger::*;` re-export block — re-exports are deferred to the consumer WO that wires tier sources into the daemon-side response-assembly pipeline (per WO-0067 deferred-re-export precedent / scope_in #58 verbatim).

9. Both new `pub fn`s (`resolve_conflict` in fusion.rs AND `merge_across_tiers` in tier_merger.rs) MUST carry rustdoc with a `# Examples` section (per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` 'Every public item has a rustdoc with `# Examples` when non-trivial'). Examples may be `no_run` doctests or pure-prose comments — either is acceptable, mirroring WO-0083 §scope_in #34 builder/handler precedent. The rustdoc on each fn MUST cite (a) the master-plan section (§18 line 1811 for resolve_conflict; §12.3 + §18 line 1813 for merge_across_tiers), (b) the feature ID (P3-W10-F10 / P3-W10-F12), (c) the WO ID (WO-0084).

10. The new types (`SourceAuthority`, `ConflictCandidate`, `ConflictResolution`, `Tier`, `TieredResult`, `MergedResult`) are `pub` (NOT `pub(crate)`) so future daemon-side consumer WOs can construct/match them without re-export gymnastics. All carry `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]` minimum; enums add `Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Copy` where appropriate (Copy on the small unit-discriminant enums Tier and SourceAuthority only; non-Copy on the data-bearing structs).

11. The TestTierSource helper IS NOT NEEDED for F12 — the merger function takes plain `&[TieredResult<T>]` slices, NOT a trait. The test constructs `TieredResult` instances inline (no trait impl needed). NB: this differs from the WO-0083 TestG4Source / WO-0070 TestG3Source pattern because the merger has no async / IO / orchestration — it's pure-deterministic data shuffling. The dependency-inversion seam for tier SOURCES (HotStagingSource / WarmTierSource / ColdTierSource that READ from real databases) is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO; the merger is purely the merge-logic surface. Production-wired tier-reader traits land in a follow-up WO that bundles the daemon-side response-assembly pipeline (after F09 + F11 ship). DO NOT introduce a TierSource trait in this WO.

12. Master-plan §15.2 tracing does NOT apply (both functions are pure-deterministic CPU-bound logic with no async / IO / spawn). Cite this explicitly in the RFR's `## Trace-span coverage` section (mirror WO-0067 §lessons_applied #5 — the deterministic-fallback module carve-out). Critic check 8 (Tracing) accepts the carve-out when explicitly disclosed.

13. Word-ban: pre-flight `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` MUST hold for production-side surface. The frozen tests (`test_conflict_resolution`, `test_multi_tier_query_merge`) live under `#[cfg(test)]` and are exempt per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out, BUT the executor MUST take the same head-of-file pre-flight scrub used in WO-0068 commit `6f6e7b9`: `head -n $(grep -n '#\[cfg(test)\]' <file> | head -1 | cut -d: -f1) <file> | grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub'` returns empty for BOTH files BEFORE first push.

14. The `unsafe` keyword MUST NOT appear in the new code. No `unsafe` blocks. No `std::env::set_var`. The merger function uses only safe APIs (Vec, slice, PartialEq, SystemTime ordering). Per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` Unsafe section.

15. **M1 mutation contract (P3-W10-F10 — fusion::resolve_conflict)**: in `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` `resolve_conflict()`, INVERT the source-authority precedence — change `.iter().map(|c| c.source).min()` to `.iter().map(|c| c.source).max()` (or analogous: invert the discriminant comparison so Text wins over LspAst). Cargo-test selector `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_conflict_resolution` MUST FAIL with the SA3 panic `(SA3) cross-tier authority precedence; left: ConflictResolution::Resolved { value: "b", source: Text, confidence: 0.99 }, right: ConflictResolution::Resolved { value: "a", source: LspAst, confidence: 0.5 }` (or analogous SA6 cross-tier dominance failure). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs`. Pre-mutation md5 snapshot: `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs > /tmp/wo-0084-fusion-orig.md5`.

16. **M2 mutation contract (P3-W10-F12 — tier_merger::merge_across_tiers)**: in `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` `merge_across_tiers()`, DROP the tier-provenance metadata — set `contributing_tiers: vec![]` unconditionally on every `MergedResult` constructed (or analogous: replace the dedup-by-value-then-collect-tiers logic with `contributing_tiers: vec![source_tier]` so the multi-tier coverage signal is lost). Cargo-test selector `cargo test -p ucil-core tier_merger::test_multi_tier_query_merge` MUST FAIL with the SA5 panic `(SA5) all-three-tiers contributing_tiers; left: [], right: [Hot, Warm, Cold]` (or analogous SA3 cross-tier same-value contributing_tiers `[Hot]` vs `[Hot, Cold]`). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs`. Pre-mutation md5 snapshot: `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs > /tmp/wo-0084-tier-merger-orig.md5`.

17. Each mutation MUST flip its targeted test from PASS to FAIL with a substantively distinct failure mode. M1 = SA3/SA6 authority-precedence inversion (boolean-precedence failure mode); M2 = SA3/SA5 tier-provenance erasure (data-projection-zeroing failure mode). Two substantively distinct surfaces — verifier accepts. Pre-restoration md5sum match confirmed for each; reality-check.sh's pre-existing-stash bug is the authoritative anti-laziness layer per WO-0072/0073/0083 lessons §verifier — `git stash` is NOT used; only `Edit` for in-place mutation + `git checkout --` for restore. Each mutation ≤ 3 lines of source change, applied/observed/restored within 60 seconds per the WO-0083 mature timing.

18. The frozen tests' SA-numbered panic messages MUST follow the format `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: <observed>; right: <expected>` per DEC-0007 + the established WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0083 pattern. Each `assert!` / `assert_eq!` carries a panic body that names the SA, identifies the semantic check, and includes both observed and expected values. Verifier reads the panic line, identifies the SA, restores the file. No grep for which test asserted what.

19. Module-coherence per DEC-0005: ONE feat commit lands the F10 fusion.rs delta (types + fn + frozen test) AND the F12 tier_merger.rs file (types + fn + frozen test) AND the lib.rs `pub mod tier_merger;` declaration as ONE cohesive unit. Splitting per-feature would produce stub-shaped intermediate states (a `pub mod tier_merger;` in lib.rs without the file would not compile; the F10 fn without the F10 frozen test would not exercise the authority-precedence; the M1/M2 mutation-restoration contract requires both surfaces to coexist in one diff). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0067 ceqp.rs (548 LOC) / WO-0068 cross_group.rs (788 LOC) / WO-0083 server.rs (982 LOC) precedent in the commit body. Estimated total LOC: ~600-800 (F10 ~150-200 + F12 ~300-400 + tests ~200).

20. Pre-flight cargo-clippy: `cargo clippy -p ucil-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` MUST be green. Pre-flight cargo-fmt: `cargo fmt --check -p ucil-core` MUST be green. The new functions MUST NOT introduce `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. Test code MAY use `.unwrap()` per the standard test-code carve-out. NB: a `.unwrap()` on `.iter().map(|c| c.source).min().unwrap()` is acceptable IF AND ONLY IF the caller has already short-circuited the empty-input path (see scope_in #2: empty input returns Unresolvable BEFORE the .min() call) — comment with a `// SAFETY: ...` line documenting the structural invariant.

21. The `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]` on data-bearing structs MUST NOT break clippy::pedantic. NB: clippy::pedantic enforces `.cloned()` over `.clone()` on iter chains; the test code that constructs `Vec<ConflictCandidate<&str>>` should use array literals with explicit field initialization rather than `vec![candidate.clone(), ...]` to avoid the pedantic warning.

22. The 22-tool catalog count is preserved (this WO does NOT touch `ucil-daemon`'s MCP tool surface). Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_all_22_tools_registered` returning green from a `cargo clean` baseline (this test was added in WO-0010 and is the canonical regression detector for the catalog count; the standing carry-forward applies — it should remain green pre+post-WO).

23. Carry-forward standing protocol (40+ WOs deep, WO-0058..WO-0083): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-core --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement. The gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-core 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-core` line in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC standing protocol.

24. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md`). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item; awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO. NOT a blocker for this WO.
25. AC convention per WO-0070/0083 lessons: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' — `git log feat/WO-0084-tier-merger-and-conflict-resolution ^main --merges` returns empty.

26. Pre-emptive carve-out: `forbidden_paths` lists `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, `ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md`, `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`. The phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-evaluator + gate-check child agents may commit refresh artefacts to `ucil-build/verification-reports/**` AND `ucil-build/escalations/**` during gate execution — those paths ARE NOT forbidden so the gate run can complete cleanly per WO-0067 scope_in #42 working template + WO-0083 carry.

27. The RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include: (a) a `## Mutation contract` section with one row per mutation (file, line range, exact patch, targeted SA, expected panic, restore command, pre-md5 path) per WO-0083 RFR template, (b) a `## Test-type effectiveness` section naming which SA caught which mutation (zero ceremonial assertions invariant), (c) a `## Disclosed deviations` section if any scope_in directive was reinterpreted (e.g., per WO-0070/0083 spirit-over-literal precedent — the executor is empowered to tighten an SA bound that does not catch the prescribed mutation, with disclosure citing the established carve-out), (d) a `## Trace-span coverage` section explicitly noting §15.2 carve-out (pure-deterministic module — no IO / async), (e) a `## DEC reference` section citing DEC-0005 module-coherence + DEC-0007 frozen-test-at-module-root + master-plan §18 line 1811 + §12.3 + §11.3 + §17 line 1636.

28. Re-export deferral: NO `pub use tier_merger::{...}` block in `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs`. NO `pub use fusion::{SourceAuthority, ConflictCandidate, ConflictResolution, resolve_conflict}` extension. The deferred re-exports land in the consumer WO that wires the response-assembly pipeline (analogous to WO-0067 § scope_in #58 deferred-re-export pattern). The `pub mod tier_merger;` declaration in lib.rs is the ONLY lib.rs change. Confirm via `git diff main -- crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs | grep -E '^\+pub use' | wc -l` returns 0.

29. Both new functions are `pub` (NOT `pub(crate)`) — the daemon-side consumer needs direct access. But neither is re-exported from lib.rs in this WO. Consumers reach them via `ucil_core::fusion::{...}` and `ucil_core::tier_merger::{...}` directly. The daemon's `Cargo.toml` already depends on `ucil-core = { workspace = true }` so this works without any new dep declaration.

30. The integration-test suite path (`tests/integration/test_tier_merger.rs` etc.) is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO. The frozen unit tests at module root in fusion.rs + tier_merger.rs are the load-bearing acceptance surface. Future integration tests that exercise the real KnowledgeGraph hot_staging tables + warm-processor outputs + memory.db cold reads land in the consumer WO per the established G-family integration-test deferral (G1 in WO-0047, G2 in WO-0050+0051, G3 in WO-0070, G4 in WO-0073/0083).
scope_out: 1. Production-wired tier sources (`HotStagingSource` / `WarmTierSource` / `ColdTierSource` impls reading from `KnowledgeGraph::stage_hot_*` / future warm-processor outputs / `memory.db`) — deferred to a follow-up WO that bundles the daemon-side response-assembly pipeline. Same shape as G1/G2/G3/G4 production-wiring deferrals.

2. Daemon-side wiring of `resolve_conflict` + `merge_across_tiers` into the response-assembly pipeline. The `ResponseAssembler` (or analogous orchestrator) that calls these functions lives in `crates/ucil-daemon/`; the wiring lands in a follow-up WO (likely the F09 quality-maximalist response assembly WO).

3. Public re-exports of `tier_merger::{...}` AND `fusion::{SourceAuthority, ConflictCandidate, ConflictResolution, resolve_conflict}` from `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs`. Deferred to the consumer WO per WO-0067 scope_in #58 precedent.

4. Warm-tier processors (P3-W10-F13). F13 depends on F12 (this WO's tier_merger) + introduces 4 rule-based processors (observation 60s / convention_signal / architecture_delta / quality_issue). F13 is the next-WO candidate.
5. F09 response-assembly + F11 bonus-context-selector (both blocked on F01 Aider repo-map). The F01-F09-F11 cascade is a separate dependency chain.

6. Cold-tier persistence semantics — the merge function takes pre-loaded `&[TieredResult<T>]` slices; how the cold-tier slice is populated (via `memory.db` SQL query, importance-decay-aware) is the consumer's responsibility per the dependency-inversion seam.
7. cargo-mutants gate per DEC-0007 (the per-WO cargo-mutants gate was removed in DEC-0007). The pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer.
8. tests/fixtures/** changes — the unit tests construct `TieredResult` / `ConflictCandidate` instances inline; no fixture mutations needed.
9. Cross-language tests (Python, TypeScript) — both features are pure-Rust in `ucil-core`.

10. New cargo workspace deps. The merger uses only `std` types (Vec, slice, SystemTime, PartialEq, Clone). NO `chrono`, NO `serde` (the types may serialize via a future serde derive in a separate WO if the response-assembly pipeline needs it; serde is NOT introduced in this WO).

11. Coverage-gate.sh sccache workaround per the standing carry-forward (40+ WOs deep). The verifier uses `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-core --tests --summary-only --json` per AC23 standing protocol. Bucket B / Bucket D candidate.
12. Effectiveness-gate.sh claude-p sub-session timeout flake (3 open escalations). Pre-existing standing scope_out, awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO. NOT a blocker.

13. `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug. Workaround: M1/M2 in-place mutation contract from the RFR is the authoritative anti-laziness layer; reality-check.sh is informational only (carry from WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073/0074/0075/0076/0077/0079/0080/0082/0083, now 14 WOs deep).

14. `unsafe std::env::set_var` is NOT NEEDED — the merger has no env-var dependency. Per WO-0076/0082 self-resolving-vs-operator-state classification, the merger is purely deterministic CPU-bound logic.
15. `tracing::instrument` annotations — the pure-deterministic module carve-out (per WO-0067 §lessons_applied #5) applies. No spans.

16. Crate-level effects on downstream crates (`ucil-daemon`, `ucil-cli`, etc.). The new types are `pub` but not re-exported from lib.rs; consumers reach them via `ucil_core::fusion::*` / `ucil_core::tier_merger::*` directly. No downstream crate Cargo.toml changes needed.
17. cargo-llvm-cov coverage on the new module being ≥ 95%. The standing AC is ≥ 85% line per AC23 / coverage-gate.sh floor. The new types + merger function will easily clear 85% via the frozen tests.
acceptance: F10 acceptance: `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_conflict_resolution` resolves uniquely (substring-match without --exact) and prints `1 passed; 0 failed; ... finished` from a `cargo clean` baseline.

F12 acceptance: `cargo test -p ucil-core tier_merger::test_multi_tier_query_merge` resolves uniquely (substring-match without --exact) and prints `1 passed; 0 failed; ... finished` from a `cargo clean` baseline.
Two new public API entry points: `ucil_core::fusion::resolve_conflict` (extends existing fusion module) AND `ucil_core::tier_merger::merge_across_tiers` (net-new module).
M1 mutation (fusion.rs authority-precedence inversion) flips fusion::test_conflict_resolution from PASS to FAIL with a substantively distinct failure mode (SA3 or SA6 panic).
M2 mutation (tier_merger.rs contributing_tiers erasure) flips tier_merger::test_multi_tier_query_merge from PASS to FAIL with a substantively distinct failure mode (SA3 or SA5 panic).
Both mutations restore via `git checkout -- <file>` and confirm md5sum match against pre-mutation snapshots.
`crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` adds exactly ONE line: `pub mod tier_merger;` placed alphabetically between `pub mod schema_migration;` and `pub mod types;` (no `pub use` re-export changes).

Coverage on `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` AND `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` ≥ 85% line via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-core --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'`.
Frozen-test selector substring-match resolves at module root for both tests per DEC-0007.
RFR document includes Mutation contract / Test-type effectiveness / Disclosed deviations / Trace-span coverage / DEC reference sections per WO-0083 template.
